mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which typically translates to higher corporate earnings and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon as economic strength permeates the market.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS year-over-year growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a historical average signals a tightening labor market, which can lead to increased consumer spending and improved corporate profits, boosting S&P 500 returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.0%, the average 20-day forward return of SPX is not positive or is statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is low and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is also low, it indicates an accommodative monetary environment that supports risk-taking and equity valuations, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative or flat on average when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are below their respective thresholds, failing to produce a positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, increasing industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a rebound in manufacturing and supply-side activity. This typically precedes stronger corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns as the economy gains momentum.
failure_condition: In post-recession recovery regimes, a positive INDPRO growth signal does not lead to statistically significant positive 20-day SPX returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) below a critical level signals a weakening in the construction sector and broader economic demand. This typically foreshadows slower economic growth and negative S&P 500 returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1000, the average 60-day forward return of SPX is not negative or is statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a healthy labor market and expanding economy. This provides a fundamental tailwind for corporate revenues and thus positive S&P 500 returns over short horizons as consumer confidence remains high.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates monetary tightening aimed at cooling the economy. This typically increases the cost of capital and negatively impacts equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: In high inflation regimes (CPIAUCSL > 3%), a rising FEDFUNDS signal does not lead to statistically significant negative 20-day SPX returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong GDP growth (GDPC1) and robust retail sales (RSAFS) indicates broad-based economic strength and consumer confidence. This provides a strong foundation for corporate profitability and positive S&P 500 returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: When both GDPC1 and RSAFS growth conditions are met, the average 20-day forward return of SPX is not positive or is statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.10).
